newsSince: No new fundamental event specific to June 9-23; the +23.5% move is momentum/analyst flow. The supportive catalysts (Washington 200G TIA availability Apr 30 for 1.6T AI-optics, Panther V storage accelerator, plus Benchmark Buy $125 / Stifel Buy $105 / Northland Outperform $110 initiations) largely predate or straddle June 9. AI-optical/interconnect thesis intact; turnaround and customer concentration remain the watch-items.

sources.label: MXL rallies on AI/5G analyst backing (StocksToTrade)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the AI-node-bottleneck lens MXL's analog/RF/edge content stays indirect versus HBM/packaging/networking cores; no new fundamental event in window makes it more central. Stays near bottom at 99. Hold, 0.
verdicts.bull: Optical/interconnect content could ride AI networking buildout.
verdicts.neutral: Indirect exposure keeps it peripheral to the node bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Turnaround story, customer concentration, far from HBM/packaging core.

verdicts.rankRationale: Most CPO-relevant listing: 200G/lane Washington TIA for 1.6T optical modules (mass production H2'26) plus sustained Buy-side backing keep the co-packaged-optics thesis intact and mildly reinforced. No fresh in-window catalyst, so +1 sentiment, hold at 36.
verdicts.bull: 200G TIA + 1.6T optics positions MXL in the AI-interconnect/CPO content wave.
verdicts.neutral: Analyst optimism mostly priced; H2'26 ramp not yet revenue.
verdicts.bear: Small-cap execution risk; optical wins still early.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master-list bucket (EDA, test & timing / design-validation) remains a poor fit for a connectivity-analog name; nothing in window strengthens or breaks that. The AI-optical momentum lives on the CPO page, not here. Hold at 96.
verdicts.bull: Broad AI/networking analog optionality if interconnect content expands.
verdicts.bear: Turnaround, concentration; weak fit to test/timing bucket.
